_comment_2: Tyk Classic API definition for the canonical p02-jwt profile (HS256 with the shared bench secret).
_comment_3: jwt_signing_method: 'hmac' selects mw_jwt.go's HMAC validation path. jwt_source carries the shared HMAC secret base64-encoded — Tyk's getSecretFromURL/getSecretToVerifySignature pipeline base64-decodes the field and treats the result as the literal HMAC secret (when it does NOT match the ^(http|https): URL regexp).
_comment_4: The decoded value 'bench-jwt-hs256-secret-2026' is the same secret listed in gateways/_reference/values.yaml#jwt.secret and gateways/_reference/jwt/secret.txt — every other gateway loads the exact same bytes.
_comment_5: jwt_identity_base_field: 'iss' makes Tyk hash on the iss claim (gateway-benchmarks for our generator) so every probe shares one ephemeral session — fine for parity since bench-default-policy applies no rate limit / quota.
_comment_6: jwt_default_policies: ['bench-default-policy'] is mandatory: without it Tyk rejects every correctly-signed token with 'no session found for token user identity'. The default policy is the permissive ACL-only shape from _policies/policies.json#bench-default-policy. We register the bench API in its access_rights below via setup.sh patching.
_comment_7: *_validation_skew: 1 (second) keeps the canonical 1 s clock skew envelope used by every other gateway (same as nginx p02-jwt). The expired-token probe uses an exp 60 s in the past, so this skew never matters operationally — it just documents intent.
name: bench
